economic_notes: Pine Creek First Nation Economic Development Corporation is accounted for by modified equity method. The corporation had revenue of $8,471,217 and earnings of $1,167,951 in 2023. Multiple capital projects underway including Waste Transfer Station, 7 new ISC homes, Daycare/Headstart Centre, CMHC Phase 19 housing, Community Hall, CMHC Phase 18 housing, Nursing Station, Bluesky Wellness Centre, McKay's Store.
notes: Auditor location is Portage la Prairie, Manitoba but firm name not fully captured in text - listed only as Chartered Professional Accountants. Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). The consolidated entity includes 4 departments (Employment and Training, Governance and Administration, Health Authority, Housing Authority CMHC) plus Pine Creek First Nation Economic Development Corporation accounted for by modified equity method. Net financial assets reported as net debt of $(6,074,880) from the statement of change in net debt. Tangible capital assets net book value not explicitly stated on balance sheet face in the extracted text. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($20,759,805) + CMHC ($375,374) + Province of Manitoba ($847,182) + Assembly of Manitoba Chiefs ($553,885) + First Peoples Development Inc. ($633,620) = $22,169,866. OSR = $27,149,364 - $22,169,866 = $4,979,498. Deferred revenue balance at year end is $6,180,449. Multiple large capital projects in progress totaling $11,877,710. Involuntary disposal of tangible capital asset resulted in a loss of $1,381,394.
